name: Manuscript Literature Framing
description: Draft LaTeX Introduction and Related Work from confirmed manuscript intent and Topic Synthesis evidence.

prompts.common: Use manuscript-literature-framing for paperTitle={{ parameter.paperTitle }}, language={{ parameter.language }}, targetVenue={{ parameter.targetVenue }}, articleType={{ parameter.articleType }}, stylePreference={{ parameter.stylePreference }}. This skill is a gate-driven staged workflow, not a direct drafting prompt. If a required Zotero or Synthesis host call is unavailable during real execution, return the legal canceled branch. Use package-local scripts/gate_runtime.py, scripts/stage_runtime.py, scripts/runtime_state.py, and references/scientific_introduction_related_work_writing_guide_zh.md. Every step starts with gate and follows only its next_action and command_example. Scripts only gate, validate, persist state, create runtime views, and write final assets; all semantic work is done by the LLM. Stage 1 clarifies and confirms manuscript intent: research problem, target object/scenario, method/system, main contributions, target venue/style, whether Related Work is separate, and preferred organization mode. Stage 2 collects materials: call synthesis.list_topics, recommend 1-5 Topic Synthesis topics, confirm material scope, then read synthesis.get_review_input for confirmed topics. Use synthesis.get_reference_sidecar_index, synthesis.get_citation_graph_metrics, synthesis.get_citation_graph_slice, synthesis.resolve_topic_paper_digest, list_library_items/search_items, item/note/payload lookup, and prepare_paper_reading_context only when needed to support citekeys, evidence checks, or ambiguity resolution. Stage 3 performs multi-angle framing analysis before any writing plan: domain/route taxonomy, timeline/timeliness, gap-to-contribution alignment, and framing synthesis. Stage 4 builds and confirms a structured writing plan; before drafting it, read references/scientific_introduction_related_work_writing_guide_zh.md. The plan must cover the Introduction functional chain, Related Work taxonomy/method lines/benchmark dimensions/debates, whether survey-of-surveys is needed, evidence/citation candidates, topic provenance, and contribution alignment. Stage 5 is final drafting: the LLM composes Introduction and Related Work LaTeX from the confirmed writing plan, evidence inventory, and framing analysis; this is not template rendering or evidence concatenation. Submit the authored draft through persist_final_draft so the script can write assets and result/result.json. Use Zotero citekeys as \cite{zotero_citekey}; never fabricate missing citekeys, use % TODO citation: paper_ref and diagnostics instead. Final artifacts are result/introduction.tex, result/related-work.tex, result/intent-brief.json, result/evidence-inventory.json, result/framing-analysis.json, result/writing-plan.json, result/citation-map.json, and result/diagnostics.json. Do not hand-write result/result.json. Final result kind must be writing.manuscript_literature_framing and include product-storage-ready metadata.
